free_text: Activation cross-sections of deuteron induced
reaction of natural Ni up to 40 MeV
High purity natNi (<99.9%, 15 microns) and NiBSi alloy
(with 90.6% natNi content, 25 microns) foils.
Activation method
and the stacked foil irradiation technique was used.
Irradiation was done with 40 MeV 70 nA deuteron beam
for 1 hour. 50 micro-m thick Al foils were used for
monitoring and energy degradation. Target foils were
individually weighed before irradiation for the
accurate determination of foil thickness. The
activities of the produced radionuclides were measured
on HPGe detectors non-destructively, without any
chemical separation. Decay data were taken from the
online nuclear decay database Nudat 2.6. Cumulative and
independent elemental cross sections were determined.
The thick target yields were measured by irradiating a
thick (600 mm) nickel foil with 21 MeV deuteron beam
for 10 min at 200 nA beam intensity in a Faraday cup.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of stopping power of
each foil due to thickness variation and straggling
effects.
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the standard way.
Uncertainty of time parameters was not included.
number of target nuclei (3%),
incident charged particle flux (7%),
nuclear decay and branching data (3%)
detector efficiency (7%)
statistical and peak fitting (3-20%)
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
Entry was checked by the corresponding author.
